reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Confirmed spec deviation. Code at protocol.rs:396-401 adds BlobsByRange to supported_protocols if fork_exists(ForkName::Deneb), which is a permanent condition (checks if Deneb epoch exists, not current epoch). Handler at rpc_methods.rs:992-1097 only validates data_availability_boundary (pruning window), not FULU_FORK_EPOCH-based deprecation. No epoch-based rejection logic exists. Spec (01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json line 770-786) explicitly requires 'respond_empty_or_reject' after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS. An attacker can trigger this by sending BlobsByRange requests after deprecation epoch, causing protocol confusion and wasted bandwidth (most requests return ResourceUnavailable due to pruning, but protocol remains advertised). Severity adjusted to Medium: impact limited by existing pruning boundaries, no consensus split or data corruption, primarily a protocol hygiene issue affecting PeerDAS migration efficiency.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: blob_sidecars_by_range must be fully deprecated after FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS. (assertion: current_epoch >= FULU_FORK_EPOCH + MIN_EPOCHS_FOR_BLOB_SIDECARS_REQUESTS => respond_empty_or_reject)
